mechanism_narrative: If FEDFUNDS is already low and CPIAUCSL is still rising, markets infer easier real policy and a longer inflation overshoot. That combination pushes the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days as term premium and expected inflation both reprice higher.
regime_hint: late-cycle disinflation with low policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL remain unchanged while the 10-year yield falls over the next 20 trading days in more than 60% of similar low-rate inflationary windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and lower future inflation pressure. Once UNRATE rises above a short-run threshold, the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days as traders price weaker growth and earlier policy easing.
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing episodes are followed by rising 10-year yields in at least 60% of cases or if the average 20-day yield change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate, the economy is generating stronger nominal demand and payroll income. That joint strength lifts expected real growth and inflation, driving the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous improvements in retail sales and payrolls do not coincide with positive 20-day yield changes, or if the sign rate falls below 40% in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO while HOUST also weakens signals supply-side cooling and a softer cyclical outlook. Under that regime, the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days because growth expectations and inflation compensation both compress.
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration in industrial production and housing starts is followed by rising 10-year yields in more than half of observed cases over a 60-trading-day evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL acceleration exceeds the recent trend, the market re-prices expected inflation upward faster than the policy rate can adjust. The 10-year Treasury yield then rises over the next 20 trading days, especially after inflation prints above the prior 3-month average.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation breakouts fail to precede yield increases, or if the average 20-day yield response is negative after CPIAUCSL crosses above its 3-month average.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS indicates labor market resilience. In that regime, traders price firmer demand and stickier inflation, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is flat or falls while PAYEMS rises month over month
failure_condition: Reject if labor-market resilience episodes produce negative 20-day yield changes more often than positive ones, or if yield responses are indistinguishable from random noise.

mechanism_narrative: If GDPC1 growth slows while RSAFS also loses momentum, final demand is weakening across both broad output and spending. That regime drives the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days because investors shift toward duration and discount weaker nominal growth.
failure_condition: Reject if paired GDP and sales slowdown episodes do not precede lower yields within 60 trading days, or if the median yield change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS rate transmits restraint only after labor and output soften. When FEDFUNDS is elevated and INDPRO is weakening, the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days as the market prices future easing and lower terminal rates.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes with weakening industrial production do not lead to lower 10-year yields, or if the sign of the 20-day yield response flips positive in tightening regimes.
